classes.reasoning: The document explicitly identifies itself as 'Protocol Amendment Summary of Changes' and is titled 'A Phase 2, multicenter, open-label study of lifileucel (tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes [TIL]) in participants with previously treated advanced melanoma.' It contains protocol number IOV-MEL-202, Amendment Number 0.1 EU-1, comprehensive sections on study objectives, design, procedures, statistical analysis, and eligibility criteria—all hallmarks of a Clinical Study Protocol Amendment.

trial_title: A Phase 2, multicenter, open-label study of lifileucel (tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes [TIL]) in participants with previously treated advanced melanoma
intervention: Lifileucel (LN-144), a tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) cell therapy administered with NMA-LD preparative regimen (cyclophosphamide and fludarabine) and aldesleukin (interleukin-2)
indication: Advanced melanoma (previously treated, unresectable or metastatic melanoma)
summary: This is the first protocol amendment (Amendment 0.1 EU-1) for study IOV-MEL-202, a Phase 2, multicenter, open-label, single-arm study evaluating lifileucel (autologous tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte cell therapy) in participants with previously treated advanced melanoma. The amendment was made in response to EU CTR Part 1 Assessment Requests and includes clarifications on the treatment setting, population definitions, benefit-risk assessment, and data security incident response procedures. The study aims to assess efficacy and safety of lifileucel in a less heavily pretreated population than prior trials, limiting prior therapy to 1 line of anti-PD-(L)1 agent with or without BRAF/MEK inhibitors.
